title: Screening host genomic projects for Wolbachia infections - Example
author: Valerio, Federica
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Nowadays, the production of full  Wolbachia  genomes does not anymore require the physical isolation of the bacterial strains from their respective hosts, and the bacterium is often sequenced as a by-catch of host genomic projects. Here, we provide an example to a step-by-step protocol to (1) identify whether host genome projects contain reads from associated  Wolbachia  and (2) isolate/retrieve the  Wolbachia  reads from the rest of the sequenced material. We hope this example will support the use of the protocol, and the completion of many projects aiming at studying diverse  Wolbachia  genome assemblies.

We hope this example will support the use of the protocol, and the completion of many projects aiming at studying diverse  Wolbachia  genome assemblies.
